MELItem.Title: Semi Lever Gear Gas Pressure/Temperature Sensors (787-10: VN-A872, VN-A873, VN-A874, VN-A875, VN-A876, VN-A877, VN-A878, VN-A879)

ListItem.Para: Gas pressure in associated semi lever gear is verified to be within the required range once each flight day.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative gas pressure/temperature sensor, and once each flight day verify the gas pressure for the associated semi lever gear is within the specified range (AMM DDG 32-30-06).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate the inoperative gas pressure/temperature sensor.
For inoperative left semi lever gear gas pressure/temperature sensor, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker MLG L STRUT-GAS SNSR and lock with an INOP tag.
For inoperative right semi lever gear gas pressure/temperature sensor, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker MLG R STRUT-GAS SNSR and lock with an INOP tag.
Verify the gas pressure for the associated semi lever gear is within the specified range.
Determine the ambient temperature of the associated semi lever gear by direct measurement of the strut.

Note.Para: If the opposite gas sensor is operative, the maintenance page temperature from that opposite operative sensor may be used to determine the temperature of the strut with the inoperative sensor.

ListItem.Para: Measure the length of exposed piston on the associated semi lever gear.
Compared the measured values for strut temperature and piston length with published tabular values to determine to the associated gas pressure for the semi lever gear strut.
If necessary, add or remove nitrogen or dry air until the strut pressure is within 20 PSI of required pressure.

Note.Para: A strut inflation tool connected to the associated semi lever gear gas service port may be used to determine the pressure in the semi lever gear.

ListItem.Para: If adjustment to gas pressure was made, wait at least 60 seconds then recheck the pressure. Repeat as needed until the pressure is within the required range.
